Keeping people in their homes : Federally-backed mortgages, including those guaranteed or insured by the Department of Veterans Affairs, are protected from foreclosure for 60 days beginning on March 18, 2020. Additionally, borrowers should be able to suspend or reduce payments for up to six months without penalties or late fees, and the lender cannot report the loan as late. Foreclosure or eviction proceedings against renters and homeowners cannot start until at least May 17 if the landlord has a government-backed mortgage. Student loan relief : Payments and interest accrual on federal student loans are suspended through September 30, as are collections on overdue loans. Loans for any semester interrupted by the virus are forgiven, and those loans and Pell grants don't count against a student's limit. Schools can continue to pay work-study students who can't work and make other emergency grants to cover students' virus-related expenses. Personal tax breaks : Withdrawals of up to $100,000 from retirement accounts without the normal 10% penalty are allowed this year for hardships related to COVID-19. A $300 charitable giving deduction without itemizing deductions is available for 2020. For those whose employers help repay their student loans, up to $5,250 of this assistance is tax-free for you in 2020. And separately from the CARES Act, state and federal income tax filing and payment deadlines have been "Federal and Maryland income tax due dates". Homelessness : Increases funding that states can use to assist families, veterans, and others experiencing homelessness. Protect jobs at large employers : To preserve jobs at large companies, some federal assistance is necessary. While this assistance should have come with greater protections for workers, Senate Democrats fought for oversight provisions that will help prevent these taxpayer funds from being abused. You can find more information on these programs and how to apply on the pages listed in Paycheck Protection Program (PPP): Zero-fee forgivable Small Business Administration (SBA) loans of up to $10 million, at 1% interest, to keep workers on payroll and pay fixed costs such as rent. (p. 2) The application window for PPP opened Friday, April 3 (April 10 for independent contractors and sole proprietors), and funds are limited. We encourage you to apply quickly through any qualified lender. "SBA Paycheck Protection Program information". SBA Debt Relief : Existing and new SBA borrowers get all loan payments covered, including principal, interest, and fees, for six months, except for PPP loans. (p. 6) Emergency Grants and Disaster Loans : A $10,000 grant can be provided to SBA disaster loan applicants. The full loan can go up to $2 million. (p. 7) Employee retention tax credit : Business tax credit worth up to $5,000 per employee for salary and health benefits paid by the hardest-hit firms. (p. 11) Payroll tax deferral : Half of 2020 payroll taxes can be deferred through the end of 2021, and half until the end of 2022. (p. 11) Eligible businesses : Small and medium businesses currently eligible for SBA programs, as well as other businesses and charitable nonprofit organizations with 500 or fewer employees. Businesses in certain industries, including hospitality, are included if they have 500 or fewer employees per location. Small business owners can also benefit from these provisions: Other tax deductions : Increased business loss and interest deductions. Fixed "retail glitch" for restaurants and retail businesses. Reimbursement for employee sick leave : Employee sick leave was covered through refundable tax credits in the Phase 2 bill. CARES provides those credits faster. Non-profit organizations can benefit from these provisions: Eligible for the above-mentioned Paycheck Protection Program, SBA emergency grant, employee retention credit, and payroll tax deferral programs. Loans : An additional Treasury loan facility for non-profits of any size (not limited to 500 employees). Disaster relief : A $45 billion Disaster Relief Fund for organizations providing critical and essential services. Deduction for donors : A special above-the-line charitable giving deduction for 2020 should also boost donations. No evictions : The state has ordered that no Maryland family or business who suffered a substantial loss of income due to COVID-19 or related closures can be evicted during the coronavirus state of emergency. The obligation to make rent and mortgage payments remains. Landlords may continue to file failure to pay rent cases, which will be considered after the state of emergency ends. No repossessions : Marylanders' cars, mobile homes, trailers, and house boats cannot be repossessed during the coronavirus emergency. The obligation to make rent and mortgage payments remains. Landlords may continue to file failure to pay rent cases, which will be considered after the state of emergency ends. Senior citizens seeking information and resources on COVID-19 can "State of Maryland information for seniors". To find school meals nearby, visit the Maryland State Department of Education's "State of Maryland school meals finder". To find child care , call Maryland's LOCATE referral system at 1-877-261-0060, or "Maryland child care information". For help paying utility bills , visit Maryland's "State of Maryland home energy assistance". For temporary cash assistance to families with dependent children, "State of Maryland cash assistance information". For state small business help (separate from the Federal programs listed above), "State of Maryland small business assistance information". "Facebook" "Twitter" "Instagram" "YouTube" |

Friend, so much uncertainty only proves what we already know: Michelle Steel is the conservative fighter we need in Washington fighting for Orange County. But we can't win this fight alone.

[**Can we count on you to chip in $1, $5, or whatever you can to put us on the path to victory?**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001JM-zPtwjHTJ8RyLxmnPU0PXwxqxfZsuzbr25EkqPhGKJo-lOvD9zWnFnKg3t7VDaXEqKAHDZcmMD91s3ugRABmaQwjruOK7sFPyK_pr7kRQA7HS4bcF0wGOR6QioFpAItgssMVBA-fPvT3JWefC9vGDmrMwa4vQ_zsMq5--RyHH25XbCxt_trR8bJWfY0Sqba-bYU0dOO6kC9uFjzB0KOXhyU7ij1Mawpg1FO7p0OiOhM2hMGnryKi-NInCYZG84rps6nDN7fHDO2wNCpMS2gWwFZUsjW-dAs6ett0-kwd7_eAVLGHYxHCuE605Q8EylNUo2dKBxG8Z5gBDY1Lgt07qgVOdOtHwMvnrbopOWmqs=&c=LO73TWZdIPbonTffJFBEhM2-YugupFt1qApWa7l8agufUQdqX6O3eg==&ch=RMrd5Gb0X9RrfDjVLXPcFONuQqmVhzAO4DcgCvuF0H6ldRWcsRTYUQ==)

Michelle has pledged to vote against any new taxes and fight to lower taxes. Even better, she has the experience to actually do it -- Michelle led the fight to return over $400 million dollars to California taxpayers.

As a first generation American, Michelle watched her single-mother who was a small business owner suffer at the hands of a broken political system that was taking advantage of those it was built to serve. Since then, she has dedicated her life to providing Californians with quick, common-sense reforms.

[**Now, we 're taking that fight to the next level and we need your help**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001JM-zPtwjHTJ8RyLxmnPU0PXwxqxfZsuzbr25EkqPhGKJo-lOvD9zWnFnKg3t7VDauqgrWmJL0jEtXKS3DatmfIpR7xFJGwMgrB3x6vY8TUd8z0rLdwkqaeZx_Uu-fv-EXwK6hU91VifQXAxNrqnDzrr3IJmnMGIFA-Ql-vM32GKiXzzJV3NHo4hUCTEGY_FlUjSPGrUQ6_zCSvKkWnVO1sugESp59iUUAD2lxmRe1suY_eRfvN3PYZA7x1ldR-sUpb5ZX2p5dRWrximPAWTQ07hehWz2MP6cS_Yl31H9GiGtu_YSQpoKl5fIW8XYUSw8r69XI3EnRMNZ0nnS44olqHPRxGDPY26_84KGKwESLYo=&c=LO73TWZdIPbonTffJFBEhM2-YugupFt1qApWa7l8agufUQdqX6O3eg==&ch=RMrd5Gb0X9RrfDjVLXPcFONuQqmVhzAO4DcgCvuF0H6ldRWcsRTYUQ==). This is going to be one of the toughest -- and most important -- races in the country. Whoever wins this seat will determine the fate of the Majority.

[**If you are able, please chip in to help us restore conservative leadership in Washington. Every donation, no matter how large or small, makes a difference.**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001JM-zPtwjHTJ8RyLxmnPU0PXwxqxfZsuzbr25EkqPhGKJo-lOvD9zWnFnKg3t7VDauqgrWmJL0jEtXKS3DatmfIpR7xFJGwMgrB3x6vY8TUd8z0rLdwkqaeZx_Uu-fv-EXwK6hU91VifQXAxNrqnDzrr3IJmnMGIFA-Ql-vM32GKiXzzJV3NHo4hUCTEGY_FlUjSPGrUQ6_zCSvKkWnVO1sugESp59iUUAD2lxmRe1suY_eRfvN3PYZA7x1ldR-sUpb5ZX2p5dRWrximPAWTQ07hehWz2MP6cS_Yl31H9GiGtu_YSQpoKl5fIW8XYUSw8r69XI3EnRMNZ0nnS44olqHPRxGDPY26_84KGKwESLYo=&c=LO73TWZdIPbonTffJFBEhM2-YugupFt1qApWa7l8agufUQdqX6O3eg==&ch=RMrd5Gb0X9RrfDjVLXPcFONuQqmVhzAO4DcgCvuF0H6ldRWcsRTYUQ==)

Thank you,

Steph

name: Tiffany Shedd
subject: TODAY at 2PM: Small Business/Payroll Protection-What you need to know with CD1 Candidate Tiffany Shedd & Congressman Markwayne Mullin (OK-2)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tiffany Shedd for Congress Committee
body:
Hello friends, **A reminder that today, Monday, April 6th, 2020 at 2:00PM I am hosting a Conference Call with Congressman Markwayne Mullin (OK-2) to address important topics affecting small business owners during this crisis and to help answer your questions. ** As a small business owner, attorney, farmer, and mom, I know all of us are facing challenging times and are trying to keep up with the quickly evolving challenges COVID-19 is having on our personal lives and businesses. Congressman Mullin has agreed to share accurate and detailed information with us to help navigate these challenges. He is extremely qualified on subjects we are all trying to figure out. As a member of the House Energy and Commerce Committee (which is one of the committees on the front lines of dealing with COVID-19) and the owner of multiple businesses ranging from plumbing to restaurants to ranching, he has valuable knowledge to share. Congressman Mullin serves on the [House Energy and Commerce Committee](http://links.sheddforcongress.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=7921d880f9b84462ad5bdb12e382a48f&_e=l97bzBAhXRgMV0wkZYTf620oI49nwsvO8oNfEyqcHYsx8IY7yfAuD2mXriSl5onKKpFuVumRyUISvG3KucckNlpJchwPoaaBff94u4enHCSnUCreqZbp-YACb0-ep5M6gLdO3bN45Akh-h7pKXw25qZCrwhERcGPvY_lrQOIuxyZawUuqcatd4rO9zkMw6wegCAqiT1tIdJ8EBceIzActVlWE9QXnO4u6cEvr86VuNpST2YtBdI5lhpMTdbMgY6XATHvZPKYBfKaC-nUnPVVkA%3D%3D) whose broad jurisdiction includes energy and environmental policy, health care, commerce, and manufacturing. Additionally, Mullin is a co-chair of the Indian Heath Services Task Force and the Vice-Chair of the Native American caucus. During his time in the U.S. House of Representatives, Mullin has been a strong voice for business owners across Oklahoma, bringing their perspective into the national debate on many issues that directly impact the business community. As one of only four Native Americans currently serving in the House, Mullin brings a firsthand knowledge of Native American issues to Washington, D.C. Mullin also serves as a member of the [Republican Whip Team](http://links.sheddforcongress.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=7921d880f9b84462ad5bdb12e382a48f&_e=l97bzBAhXRgMV0wkZYTf6_zbjaoD8oKqNFW5W0_xOowu58FShRLL52L65lPxAw2e65i22T73Dg0Ke2eXezXUfZ1nsyQk5eMxVyVTh3eS5uOL1QKaQcrNhd_EsQOxSBtHMxZtV0oqrKhsyVaQ_SMhZ6my7iE_CBwxndOo-zt350ErckO9UVGIFPg-ohOnmdUUmfGGnB_TYLcAk7DXVHpmTDLbKKRH1gZ9q3yzrmtX6CvV1K7hwhScbTBzLqkVyeTAtm-QgJMzhQ0G_yw9AkBiVA%3D%3D). Representatives who are members of this team work closely with Republican leadership to ensure every American’s voice is heard in Congress. I hope you can join us, and please feel free to share this invitation with anyone you think would benefit by this call. **Date:** Monday, April 6th
**Time: ** 2:00PM to 3:00PM AZ Time As a candidate for Arizona CD1, I am talking to many people across the state. CD1 is a vast, diverse district. When I realized many people are seeking information that I have access to, I set up this call to share knowledge with you. For more information about my campaign, please visit: [www.sheddforcongress.com](http://links.sheddforcongress.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=7921d880f9b84462ad5bdb12e382a48f&_e=SWDn6vOt7VN4jslWFO2UrOUNFcwjGDUro5ajKCD1x9bKrDq4Gnz5TDBRJ0hMBBdNVGXYtiJk4_6g7ANUODZwBimNeEgKa5Fn_TkNUIHUMLIQH529AYMuZ1D_BGZggMrE5Bn7c_BKO53gejdHaqJiTlGQRM4TArsgzRnOaESyJFj_LsMQXO5RRX__EryUv0DTqEAJ--_XAkiX9jJMqNmeNGOPFO0rPbYO5yjq_O3AK--4QFIrQilade399pSg-iC_QhG6-cAw7WBjdFYDjc5hIA%3D%3D). ***Please confirm your participation by replying to this email. If you have already confirmed, please consider this a reminder.*** I look forward to talking with you at 2:00PM today. Warm Regards, Tiffany Shedd Shedd for Congress
111 West Florence Blvd., Suite 7
Casa Grande, Arizona 85122
[Info@sheddforcongress.com](mailto:info@sheddforcongress.com "info@sheddforcongress.com")
520-689-4520
[www.sheddforcongress.com](http://links.sheddforcongress.com/u/click?_t=aef90635dd7e4e83bfd7b3a79f893ad5&_m=7921d880f9b84462ad5bdb12e382a48f&_e=SWDn6vOt7VN4jslWFO2UrOUNFcwjGDUro5ajKCD1x9bKrDq4Gnz5TDBRJ0hMBBdNBArD9c0uU0mnNtGHg3v4KMX5iUfIDcCSBg4BfkPfsxNezTid2ZYt7K4rQtLoS5iDQJARXqQ_dnSazy0g8XY8ZbLgywolbLAvkEUuUp4If8lL0H0IwyfXNGn6QEeEeQTLTOFk8rCWnZ2irQcWOk2J4EnaJ76eA-3yroYyvMQ_-oKx_y1tXAXOaD-8YL6bjF7_ZRt8Cbm8h83jCEdlh4Rdnw%3D%3D)

Paid for by Tiffany Shedd for Congress Committee

Have you heard the news? Former Speaker Bill O’Brien has endorsed me! Read More >>
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2359cdf1ab3920e0/e240cee8583f63d4c3d6b838967d376c/33153) Peter, Former New Hampshire House Speaker Bill O’Brien has officially endorsed my campaign for U.S. Senate!

I’m honored to have Bill’s support. He has long served this state and its citizens, and his record of public service is truly inspiring.

With his support, I will continue my efforts to return New Hampshire's values and conservative traditions to the U.S. Senate by defeating Jeanne Shaheen this fall.

[**Will you join Speaker O’Brien right now by rushing an urgent endorsement contribution? Just click or tap here**.](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2359cdf1ab3920e0/91fee8891693ee27c3d6b838967d376c/33153)

I know Speaker O’Brien well, and he does not take an endorsement like this lightly. In his own words, “I’ve talked to [Corky] about having borders that mean something. He understands that we have to get our fiscal house in order. I don’t believe he’s just looking for his next job. He could be in a personal position to be comfortable and step back, but he has a sense of community and civil service... Corky is a real conservative.” [ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2359cdf1ab3920e0/7703930cc8a12072c3d6b838967d376c/33153) Jeanne Shaheen is much more radical than many people realize. She consistently backs her party’s leadership like Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer – even when it hurts Granite Staters.

Speaker O’Brien knows that I’m the best candidate suited to DEFEAT D.C. Shaheen, and return New Hampshire’s values and conservative traditions to the U.S. Senate this fall.

Please, consider [**rushing a contribution right now to help me fight for our values, and fire Jeanne Shaheen**.](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/2359cdf1ab3920e0/4ee20d9a2b66786ec3d6b838967d376c/33153)

disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
| |

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16964705/180742129/1003218987?refcode=em040620-header&refcode2=2cfff9da-5778-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyY2ZmZjlkYS01Nzc4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5eKM66hSadRfWwwzTO21B5zFR1shWFdyFP_QYwEdxBA=) **** | | Friend,

**We have reached a time when a rigid adherence to ideology may well cost us lives. For 40 years, a dangerous anti-government streak has crept into the Republican Party, and our own Georgia Governor Kemp demonstrated it in the face of the coronavirus. He chose to delay and deny, because he could not think of how a coordinated government response to the pandemic could save lives and provide the quickest route to community and economic recovery.**

So when Governor Kemp claimed not to understand asymptomatic transmission of coronavirus until late last week, Teresa saw through his excuse and blasted him, **not for his ignorance, but for his cowardice. You see, if you don’t believe in government, you can’t run it — and Kemp was too afraid to look his constituents in the eye and tell them that his Republican swagger — his “don’t tread on me” go-it-aloneism — had put Georgians’ lives at risk.**

Meanwhile, his appointed United States Senator appears to be implicated in insider stock trading, and there are questions about David Perdue’s stock trades in casinos and pharmaceuticals following a January 24th Senate briefing, as well. Teresa blasted anyone who would seek to manipulate the system for their own self-interest at a time of national crisis as “gamers” who do not deserve to serve.

**Teresa served for two terms as the mayor and public safety director of Columbus, Georgia, leading the city to become one of the Top 25 Best Run Cities in the country. She is the kind of leader we need in a time of crisis — not these gamers who look out for themselves when they should be working for the people.**

**[Watch her weekend interview on MSNBC and then pitch in if you're ready for real leadership from Georgia's United States senator.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16964706/180742130/1003218987?refcode=em040620-b&refcode2=2cfff9da-5778-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyY2ZmZjlkYS01Nzc4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5eKM66hSadRfWwwzTO21B5zFR1shWFdyFP_QYwEdxBA=)** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16964707/180742131/1003218987?refcode=em040620-b&refcode2=2cfff9da-5778-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyY2ZmZjlkYS01Nzc4LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5eKM66hSadRfWwwzTO21B5zFR1shWFdyFP_QYwEdxBA=) —Team Tomlinson

name: Tina Smith
subject: An explanation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
Hello, I am about to ask you to add a contribution to my re-election campaign — but first, you deserve to know exactly how this pandemic will affect our work moving forward. I hope you’ll give me a moment to explain something. Our campaign has always emphasized making grassroots, person-to-person connections. That’s how we won our race in 2018, and it’s how we can win again — but we’ll need to do a few things differently this time around. **This new reality may have caused us to change our campaign strategy and move everything online, but our critical work to save Minnesota’s Senate seat and flip the majority is far from over.** As we rely more and more on emails just like this one to sustain our movement, my team will be working around the clock to make sure we’re stretching every one of your hard-earned dollars as far as we can. But the way things are currently looking, we will fall behind on our fundraising target for the month. This doesn’t come as a surprise to anyone on my team given the circumstances. It does mean, however, that we will need to work twice as hard to make up for it. **So, what do you say? Can you chip in a donation of $10 or any amount you can afford to support our re-election campaign today? These are difficult times, so I only want you to pitch in if you are financially able to at this time. clean_body: | Hello, I am about to ask you to add a contribution to my re-election campaign — but first, you deserve to know exactly how this pandemic will affect our work moving forward. I hope you’ll give me a moment to explain something. Our campaign has always emphasized making grassroots, person-to-person connections. That’s how we won our race in 2018, and it’s how we can win again — but we’ll need to do a few things differently this time around. This new reality may have caused us to change our campaign strategy and move everything online, but our critical work to save Minnesota’s Senate seat and flip the majority is far from over. As we rely more and more on emails just like this one to sustain our movement, my team will be working around the clock to make sure we’re stretching every one of your hard-earned dollars as far as we can. But the way things are currently looking, we will fall behind on our fundraising target for the month. This doesn’t come as a surprise to anyone on my team given the circumstances. It does mean, however, that we will need to work twice as hard to make up for it. So, what do you say? Can you chip in a donation of $10 or any amount you can afford to support our re-election campaign today? These are difficult times, so I only want you to pitch in if you are financially able to at this time. Thank you in advance. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = We face an uncertain and unprecedented time, but knowing you opened this email makes me so grateful. Thank you for continuing to engage with our campaign and the incredible work we are doing to take back the Senate. I really mean it. We will get through this together. Stay safe. — Tina P.S. I know this is a difficult time for everyone, so please stay safe out there. To receive fewer emails during this public health emergency,_ Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota Tina Smith for Minnesota PO Box 14362 St. Paul, MN 55114 United States Contributions or gifts to Tina Smith for Minnesota are not tax-deductible.

# _**President Donald J. Trump ****NEEDS OUR HELP.**_
Our great president has been taking a lot of undeserved heat from the do nothing democrats during this crisis. They are calling for another House investigation into how well the president handled this crisis. If they succeed, it will simply be another partisan hit job on the most transparent president in modern history. These democrat-led sham investigations are why our President desperately needs Congressmen and Congresswomen that will suppourt his agenda in the people’s house. This is why our campaign is so important to the future of the country. We need someone that has proven they can win a general election in a democrat-held District that has been in Congress before to immediately help support the Trump Agenda. Please help make this a reality and support our movement by donating, volunteering, or even telling your friends about our campaign. Continue staying safe from Covid-19.

God bless the United States of America,

\- Bobby Schilling

name: Team Kathy
subject: It's long past time to expand medicaid
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
Kathy wants to know what you think.
|
---

| | It's long past time: North Carolina must expand Medicaid so hardworking North Carolinians can receive the health care they need -- right now during the current crisis and every other day, too. [_**Do you support expanding Medicaid in North Carolina?**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d37b/3d199a51/2f7a71e8/7364f265/2251623675/VEsH/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/e200406_nca") | | | [YES](secure.kathymanningfornc.com/e200406_nca)
---
| | [no](secure.kathymanningfornc.com/e200406_nca)
---
There are more than _30 million_ uninsured people in our country. They're rightfully concerned about getting sick as a result of the coronavirus outbreak, but even before the pandemic, _more than half_ of Americans were afraid to go to the doctor because of the astronomical cost of care.
**No one should have to choose between getting the care they need and putting food on the table.**
We need to protect the health of our communities -- especially frontline workers like health care providers, store clerks, and farmers who are keeping our country going right now.
Expanding Medicaid through the Affordable Care Act would allow more than 194,000 North Carolinians -- who aren't eligible under current requirements but might not earn enough to purchase their own health plans -- to receive coverage.
[_**Kathy wants to know what you think about this important issue. Take our poll and tell us if you support expanding Medicaid for hundreds of thousands of North Carolinians ->**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d37b/3d199a51/2f7a71e8/7364f262/2251623675/VEsE/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "http://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/e200406_nca")
Thanks,
Team Kathy

---

---

---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Will=20Douglas=20for=20Texas=20House?=
subject: Campaign Update
body:
As Texas continues to fight against our invisible enemy I can't help but be overwhelmed by the acts of charity and selflessness that I've seen | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/willdouglasfortexas/campaign-update-april-6-2020?e=4f010155df)
---
| | [ ](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=2b4a91f1b5&e=4f010155df)
---
| | Friends,

As Texas continues to fight against our invisible enemy I can't help but be overwhelmed by the acts of charity and selflessness that I've seen across the state: neighbors helping neighbors, communities coming together, and people putting their lives & businesses at risk to help others.

I have been laser-focused on providing care to patients at my two pharmacies while my political efforts have shifted focus to social media and technology platforms, and we're now in the sixth month of this campaign we launched last fall.

At this half-year mark, I thought we could take a look back at what we've accomplished so far:
* In October, I [launched](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=47d3cb0d46&e=4f010155df) my campaign against incumbent Democrat Rhetta Bowers in Texas House District 113.
* In December, I officially [qualified](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=c38203bd53&e=4f010155df) as the Republican nominee of HD 113 with no primary opponent, meaning I will be able to focus all of our resources on beating Rhetta Bowers in November.
* After the New Year, the latest TEC reports came out and it was [revealed](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=8640f8f5aa&e=4f010155df) I easily out-raised my Democrat opponent and have more cash on hand.
* In January and February, I was endorsed by [Governor Greg Abbott, Associated Republicans of Texas](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=9a95e6e353&e=4f010155df), [Texans for Lawsuit Reform PAC](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=39c5b32a02&e=4f010155df), and the [Dallas Police Association](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=a0cd2b2c52&e=4f010155df).
* In March, due to the coronavirus, I temporarily [suspended](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=b389f36942&e=4f010155df) all public events for my campaign to focus on my pharmacies and caring for our community.

It's been an honor to run, and I'm looking forward to continue campaigning strongly through November. See below for a brief recap of our past week...
---
|
---
|
---
Due to a shortage of hand sanitizer available at stores, the FDA recently loosened regulations to allow pharmacists like myself to compound certain alcohol-based hand sanitizer. We're now churning out product for our customers using Everclear.
|
---
| [ ](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=d529cd720b&e=4f010155df)
---
Earlier this week I gave a brief video update on how our pharmacies (and campaign) is adapting because of the coronavirus. Please click through to watch.
|
---
| _**News Updates on COVID-19**_
* **Global:**[Total](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=4ef567a8d5&e=4f010155df) confirmed cases as of 2pm: 1,309,439 \-- Total deaths: 73,703.
* **U.S.:** [Total](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=b4daf77f93&e=4f010155df) confirmed cases as of 2pm: 352,546 \-- Total deaths: 10,389.
* **Texas** : [Total](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=ecc516699b&e=4f010155df) confirmed cases as of 2pm: 7,276 -- Total deaths: 140.
* **Federal government update:** President Trump [tweeted](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=0ce3837de5&e=4f010155df) Monday that there is a “light at the end of the tunnel,” following a series of grim warnings about this week’s expected death toll from coronavirus.
* **Stock Market update:** On Monday, stocks [surged](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=c0569bde62&e=4f010155df) as reports surfaced that the coronavirus epidemic plaguing New York may be slowing down.
* **Other resources:** Texas DSHS on [how to avoid the virus](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=0166efa53c&e=4f010155df), [what to do if you get it](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=1f0c72e00a&e=4f010155df).

---
|
---
| _**This Upcoming Week**_
---
| [ ](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=60925e5707&e=4f010155df)
---
| We will continue to campaign and raise money in unconventional ways, because it's imperative come November that we have the support and resources necessary to defeat a first-term Democrat incumbent in a long-time Republican seat.
* To tune in to a Facebook Live Townhall on COVID-19 that I'll be hosting tomorrow at 4:00pm with Dallas County Commissioner J.J. Koch, please [CLICK HERE](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=26b68d445e&e=4f010155df) to register a reminder on Facebook.
* To contribute to my campaign for State House, please [CLICK HERE](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=0b878254c6&e=4f010155df) or send a check to "Douglas for State House" at PO Box 496955, Garland, TX 75043.

Stay healthy and stay safe. We will get through this.

For Texas,

Will Douglas
Republican Nominee for TX-HD-113
---
|
---
| [CONTRIBUTE](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=dcf514cb80&e=4f010155df "CONTRIBUTE")

Will Douglas for TexasPO Box 496955Garland, TX 75049-6955

name: Leah, MJ Hegar Digital
subject: Digital tools - what we need
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
Due to COVID-19, the campaign is working from home and all in-person events are suspended.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903b9/7f51f7bc/4c84efab/4ef132af/1197051285/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: By the numbers: A massive nationwide response
body:
When the world has faced trying times, Americans have never failed to rise to the occasion. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cf7e9ec2ad&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 6, 2020
---
| | |
# **By the numbers: A massive nationwide response**

**When the world** has faced trying times, Americans have never failed to rise to the occasion. The Coronavirus pandemic is and will be no exception.

**🇺🇸** [Sparing no expense to win this fight](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a58b7f466e&e=3ee4985eea)

**“We have the best** doctors, the best military leaders, and the best logistics professionals anywhere in the world,” President Trump said yesterday. “And we’re orchestrating a massive federal response unlike anything our country has ever seen or done.”

**Here’s what just part** of that response looks like, by the numbers:

* By tomorrow, the Federal Government will have deployed **over 3,000 military and public health professionals** to New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, and other parts of our country most affected by Coronavirus.

* In the last 7 days, FEMA has airlifted crucial supplies and protective equipment from every corner of the Earth. Since last Sunday, cargo planes have delivered nearly **300 million gloves, almost 8 million masks, and 3 million gowns**.

* The government is delivering an additional **600,000 N95 masks to New York City** to support its public hospital system, as requested by Mayor Bill de Blasio. Another 200,000 will be delivered to Suffolk County on Long Island.

* Over the last 48 hours, FEMA has delivered an additional **500 ventilators to New Jersey**. In addition, Louisiana has received 200, Michigan has received 300, and Illinois has gotten or will get 600 to address potential hotspots.

* As of yesterday, the United States has tested and given results to **1.67 million people** —far more than any other country on Earth.

**In addition** to these public health actions, President Trump has worked with Congress to prioritize the economic needs of American workers and small businesses. The [$2+ trillion CARES Act](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8227bbcd54&e=3ee4985eea) signed into law on March 27 provides qualifying families with $2,400 in tax-free payments, along with an additional $500 for each child.

**The Paycheck Protection Program** , which launched Friday, approved more than 17,500 loans valued at over $5.4 billion on its first day. These loans, intended to keep more Americans employed, are 100% forgivable if used for qualifying expenses _and_ if all employees are kept on payroll.

**America is thankful** to every family making sacrifices for the good of our country during this time—and especially to the healthcare workers fighting this virus and saving lives each and every day.

**“In the days ahead,** America will endure the peak of this terrible pandemic,” President Trump said. “Our warriors in this life-and-death battle are the incredible doctors and nurses and healthcare workers on the frontline of the fight. We pledge to them our eternal gratitude and everlasting support.”

🎬 [We may be apart for Holy Week, but now is the time for the power of prayer](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5e417cb541&e=3ee4985eea)

**VP Pence:** [There’s light at the end of the tunnel, thanks to the American people](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6048a0b756&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
| **U.S. Surgeon General Jerome Adams delivers remarks at a Coronavirus briefing** | April 3, 2020
---
|

---

name: Cory Booker
subject: The biggest thing you can do today is a small act of kindness
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CORY BOOKER FOR SENATE
body:
Mr. Rogers said "Look for the helpers." I looked, and here's what I found. |
#
Friends, In the darkest of times, I always turn to the power of community. Mr. Rogers once said, "When I was a boy and I would see scary things in the news, my mother would say to me, 'Look for the helpers. You will always find people who are helping.'" So, I looked: [](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0e7d/5fca0d19/7a7d3233/291d144b/1926254622/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVkRhWFI1SlNVPSI6IkNyZXNjbyIsIkpTVlRWRUZVUlY5QlFrSlNKU1U9IjoiUEEiLCJKU1ZhYVhBbEpRPT0iOiIxODMyNiJ9/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/april-2020-share-your-story?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bookerlive&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em200406&source=em200406") And, team, what I found was inspiring to say the least. Stories of health care workers displaying boundless compassion for their patients. Stories of residents taking care of their elderly neighbors by delivering prepared meals or just checking in from time to time. Simple statements of gratitude for grocery store clerks and postal workers. Acts and feats of selflessness and kindness in all shapes and sizes. **Here are a few of my favorites:** [](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0e7d/5fca0d19/7a7d3233/291d144a/1926254622/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVkRhWFI1SlNVPSI6IkNyZXNjbyIsIkpTVlRWRUZVUlY5QlFrSlNKU1U9IjoiUEEiLCJKU1ZhYVhBbEpRPT0iOiIxODMyNiJ9/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/april-2020-share-your-story?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bookerlive&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=em200406&source=em200406")

[](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0e7d/5fca0d19/7a7d3233/291d1449/1926254622/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVkRhWFI1SlNVPSI6IkNyZXNjbyIsIkpTVlRWRUZVUlY5QlFrSlNKU1U9IjoiUEEiLCJKU1ZhYVhBbEpRPT0iOiIxODMyNiJ9/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/april-2020-share-your-story?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bookerlive&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=em200406&source=em200406")

It is a challenging time for all. And this is far more than a campaign, more than an email listserv -- this is a movement, a community, a family.
So if you're comfortable sharing the story of a hero in your life, I'd be honored to hear about it. Or if you simply have a message of gratitude for others on the frontlines of this pandemic, I'd love to hear those, too.
We are all in this together. I hope you're staying safe and I can't wait to read your responses.
Remember, the biggest thing you can do today is a small act of kindness for someone else.
Be well,
Cory

clean_body: Mr. Rogers said "Look for the helpers." I looked, and here's what I found. | Friends, In the darkest of times, I always turn to the power of community. Mr. Rogers once said, "When I was a boy and I would see scary things in the news, my mother would say to me, 'Look for the helpers. You will always find people who are helping.'" So, I looked: "" And, team, what I found was inspiring to say the least. Stories of health care workers displaying boundless compassion for their patients. Stories of residents taking care of their elderly neighbors by delivering prepared meals or just checking in from time to time. Simple statements of gratitude for grocery store clerks and postal workers. Acts and feats of selflessness and kindness in all shapes and sizes. Here are a few of my favorites: "" "" "" "" "" "" | "" It is a challenging time for all. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: I’m excited to share this
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
I’m excited to share some really positive news for El Paso.

The Paul Foster Foundation and Woody and Gayle Hunt Foundation have
pledged a $1 million matching grant for the El Paso food bank.

Our local food bank (known as El Pasoans Fighting Hunger) is seeing
unprecedented demand for food in the midst of this extraordinary
contraction of our economy. In a county where more than 200,000 people
were already food insecure, there are now tens of thousands more in El
Paso without work, who can no longer afford to feed themselves.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42423?refcode=ema20200406-84466&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1019&akid=84466%2E444211%2EtrI52a ]Can you join me in making a donation to El Pasoans Fighting Hunger?
Every donation will be matched by the foundations. That means for every $1
you give today, the food bank gets $2.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42423?refcode=ema20200406-84466&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=84466%2E444211%2EtrI52a ]Contribute $3

The demands on the food bank are the greatest we’ve ever seen. This
matching grant could not have come at a better time.

But it also means that we all need to step up and do our part. The $1
million pledge is a match, which means that an additional $1 million must
be raised to realize the full pledged amount.

If we succeed, it will result in a total of $2 million raised, which will
mean that every hungry family in El Paso will be able to get the nutrition
and calories they need to survive.

I know we can do this. El Paso is a generous, giving community. We’ve
always been there for each other. Most recently, we raised millions of
dollars after the Walmart shooting to provide medical care and financial
support for the families who were affected.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42423?refcode=ema20200406-84466&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=84466%2E444211%2EtrI52a ]This is another opportunity for us to show each other who we are and
what we are capable of doing. I hope you will join me in making a donation
to the El Paso food bank.

Thank you,

Beto

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42423?refcode=ema20200406-84466&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=84466%2E444211%2EtrI52a ]Contribute

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1023&akid=84466%2E444211%2EtrI52a

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Stay hunkered down, Hoosiers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
We’re going to get through these next two weeks. We’ll look back on these two
weeks and realize we did the right things

<>
Friend,

“What we do today, what we do tomorrow, is going to have a direct impact on
where we are two weeks from now.” –Governor Holcomb

Today Governor Holcomb officially extended Indiana’s stay-at-home executive
order for another two weeks. The new Executive Order also sets out specific
guidelines for retail businesses.

Retail business that provide necessities of life may remain open if they:

* Limit the number of customers in a store at any one time.
* Adjust hours to accommodate vulnerable populations, such as the elderly.
* Limit hours so they can restock and clean.
* Comply with all mitigation measures to protect employees and customers.

Other retail businesses may remain open if they restrict sales to online or
call-in ordering with delivery or curbside pickup. Examples of retail stores
that do not provide necessities of life are (but not limited to) bookstores,
craft and fabric stores, beauty supply stores and florists.

Additionally, Governor Holcomb announced all campgrounds will be closed except
to those who use recreational vehicles or cabins as their primary residence.
This includes campgrounds within Indiana State Parks. However,state parks will
remain open to daily visitors.

“We have this in us. I’m seeing it all over the state of Indiana. There are
so many people putting their communities, their neighbors, our entire state
ahead of themselves.”

Governor Holcomb and his COVID-19 task-force project that we are at the very
beginning of the COVID-19 surge.

This prohibition of large gatherings is difficult, especially during Holy
Week. But, as Governor Holcomb said, the Church is a body, not a building. He’s
grateful to all the faith leaders who arestreaming their services online so
members can continue to practice their faith, while staying safe and healthy at
home.

This virus does not discriminate, it does not care what crowd you’re in or how
you practice your faith, if you do at all. We need to take every possible
measure we can to mitigate risk, slow the spread and flatten the curve.

“We’re going to get through these next two weeks. We’ll look back on these two
weeks and realize we did the right things. And when I say “we,” I don’t mean
state government, I mean all of us. We the people.”

#InThisTogether

-Your Holcomb Crew

Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana. © Copyright 2020. All rights reserved.

Eric Holcomb for Indiana, 101 W Ohio Street, Suite 2200, Indianapolis, IN
46204 | (317) 569-0709

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

Unsubscribe

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Stay hunkered down, Hoosiers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
We’re going to get through these next two weeks. We’ll look back on these two
weeks and realize we did the right things

<>
Friend,

“What we do today, what we do tomorrow, is going to have a direct impact on
where we are two weeks from now.” –Governor Holcomb

Today Governor Holcomb officially extended Indiana’s stay-at-home executive
order for another two weeks. The new Executive Order also sets out specific
guidelines for retail businesses.

Retail business that provide necessities of life may remain open if they:

* Limit the number of customers in a store at any one time.
* Adjust hours to accommodate vulnerable populations, such as the elderly.
* Limit hours so they can restock and clean.
* Comply with all mitigation measures to protect employees and customers.

Other retail businesses may remain open if they restrict sales to online or
call-in ordering with delivery or curbside pickup. Examples of retail stores
that do not provide necessities of life are (but not limited to) bookstores,
craft and fabric stores, beauty supply stores and florists.

Additionally, Governor Holcomb announced all campgrounds will be closed except
to those who use recreational vehicles or cabins as their primary residence.
This includes campgrounds within Indiana State Parks. However,state parks will
remain open to daily visitors.

“We have this in us. I’m seeing it all over the state of Indiana. There are
so many people putting their communities, their neighbors, our entire state
ahead of themselves.”

Governor Holcomb and his COVID-19 task-force project that we are at the very
beginning of the COVID-19 surge.

This prohibition of large gatherings is difficult, especially during Holy
Week. But, as Governor Holcomb said, the Church is a body, not a building. He’s
grateful to all the faith leaders who arestreaming their services online so
members can continue to practice their faith, while staying safe and healthy at
home.

This virus does not discriminate, it does not care what crowd you’re in or how
you practice your faith, if you do at all. We need to take every possible
measure we can to mitigate risk, slow the spread and flatten the curve.

“We’re going to get through these next two weeks. We’ll look back on these two
weeks and realize we did the right things. And when I say “we,” I don’t mean
state government, I mean all of us. We the people.”

#InThisTogether

-Your Holcomb Crew

46204 | (317) 569-0709
